title: Vitiligo

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting lesion site and distribution, clinician-established type, extent, stability, speed, triggers, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, and personal or family autoimmune and thyroid context.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact history and impact section supports documenting a vitiligo context, onset, extent, stability or progression, triggers, function, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, concerns, clinician skin inspection and distribution, and prior records without diagnosing vitiligo.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting lesion site and distribution, clinician-established type, extent, stability, speed, triggers, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, and personal or family autoimmune and thyroid context.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with vitiligo 2021

exact_section.heading: Recommendations R1, R3, and R5 — history, extent, stability, impact, and associated disease

follow_up.text: Supports recording medical photography and documented affected areas for clinician-led monitoring without creating a response conclusion or fixed interval.
follow_up.rationale: The exact monitoring section supports documenting clinical photographs reviewed when available and consented without inventing progression or response.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports recording medical photography and documented affected areas for clinician-led monitoring without creating a response conclusion or fixed interval.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with vitiligo 2021

sources_used.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with vitiligo 2021
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Exact type, body-surface extent, stability, progression rate, triggers, autoimmune and thyroid history, psychosocial assessment, diagnosis, laboratory testing, treatment, medicine, phototherapy, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
